Falling for his friend’s son had never been part of the plan. Neither was discovering you again on a dating app—meant to escape the thought of you.

Damian Lugo was born into wealth, heir to a family empire built on luxury hotels. 

From a young age, men threw themselves at him—hungry for his money, his power and his reputation as a notorious playboy. Damian indulged them, reveling in the excess for as long as he could remember.

That is, until the night your father called to tell him he’d suffered a stroke.

Damian and Derrick had been inseparable since college, so Damian was among the first at his hospital bedside. It was there he saw Sandra—standing vigil and nursing her husband back to health.

The image burrowed deep into Damian, unsettling him more than he cared to admit.

For the first time, Damian recognized the hollow ache in his own life. He didn’t want another nameless body in his bed, another fleeting thrill.

He wanted someone who would stay. Someone who would love him, not his fortune.

But the city had never been kind in offering him that. Everyone wanted a piece of Damian Lugo—the money, the power, the name. No one wanted him.

No one, until the day he saw you—Derrick’s son. The one person he could never dare to touch, yet the only one he found himself unable to resist.
